source.title: What's the Legislative Process for the Star Atlas DAO
source.author: ATMTA / Star Atlas official publisher

summary.archival_abstract: Official support article titled “What's the Legislative Process for the Star Atlas DAO.” This record preserves what Star Atlas Support / ATMTA publicly stated at the recorded publication time; claims about delivery, economics, or outcomes remain limited to the wording of the source.
summary.historical_value: Official evidence baseline for later reconciliation with community reporting.
summary.current_validity_warning: Official publication does not independently prove successful execution, completeness, or continued current validity.

article_text:
Idea Submission: A DAO member submits an idea for a PIP (Proposal Improvement Proposal) to #pip-ideas in the Star Atlas Discord and discusses it.
Community Feedback: The PIP idea gets feedback and support from the community.
Form Submission: A DAO member fills out the form linked from the governance channels on Discord.
Draft Posting: The PIP goes up on the #pip-drafts channel.
Council Assignment: A bot assigns the draft to a Star Atlas Council Member.
Council Review: The PIP is discussed and approved/rejected by the Council.
Foundation Submission: An approved draft is sent to the Star Atlas Foundation.
Foundation Review: The Star Atlas Foundation approves or rejects the PIP.
Voting: If approved by the Star Atlas Foundation, the PIP goes up for voting on govern.staratlas.com.
Public Debate: The PIP is posted in #pip-discussion to allow for public debate while voting is open.
DAO Vote: The PIP is either approved or rejected by the Star Atlas DAO members.
Implementation: If approved, the PIP is sent back to the Star Atlas Foundation for implementation.
